value.expression: Query JSON Session to retrieve values at the root level by just giving the key name. Here's the output from referencing token: $sJSONQueryReturn$
Query nested objects using dot notation. Here's the output from querying user.email: $sJSONQueryReturn$
Count objects in an array by using .length() at the end of any array object (note: the length is returned as string). Here's the output from querying user.roles.length(): $sJSONQueryReturn$

Count objects in an array by using .length() at the end of any array object can also let you know when an array is empty. Here's the output from querying user.sysAssignedRoles.length(): $sJSONQueryReturn$
Arjun Query objects within an array by referencing the position of the target object. Here's the output from querying user.roles[0].name: $sJSONQueryReturn$
Since the object positioning is 0 based, remeber the second object would be in position 1. Here's the output from querying user.roles[1].name: $sJSONQueryReturn$
A full array can be returned as string by referencing the array itself using dot notation. Here's the output from querying user.roles: $sJSONQueryReturn$
